title: Build like it's 2030. Listen like you mean it.
key_points: A YC partner told Dwyer that any company founded before 2023 is now considered 'legacy', without an AI-native team, product, and growth rate, customers leave, you can't raise money, and no one will acquire you.

Core thesis: differentiation in an era where everyone uses the same models (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini) comes from velocity PLUS insight. Velocity without insight just ships slop; insight without velocity makes you irrelevant.
Cited Anthropic data: Claude Code output grew 8x since 2023, and roughly 65% of code is now deployed to production with no human interaction, rising sharply each quarter.

Great Question rebuilt as AI-native by rethinking time (PRDs that took 3-5 days now take a morning), making everyone a product builder (support reps ship fixes directly), and shifting from gates-up-front to 'permissive by design' with design-system guardrails (Zeroheight) and review rituals.

Demoed a Figma Make dashboard feeding into Great Question via MCP: ship a prototype, collect real customer feedback with verbatim quotes (anti-hallucination), then push it back to Figma Make to auto-generate a new version, closing the research loop in real time. The differentiator is the 'last 10%': taste, the quality bar, and the knowledge problems you have the right to solve.

quotes.text: if we take velocity, which is critical to the future, but we deliver it without insight, all you're really doing is delivering slop.
quotes.talk: Build like it's 2030. Listen like you mean it.

quotes.text: design isn't dead just like great question, it's being reborn.
quotes.talk: Build like it's 2030. Listen like you mean it.
